fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Channeled Scablands: basalt ripples read as ‘weird fields’—use aerial only where legal.
2️⃣ Grand Coulee: low-frequency dam rumble records well—place mic off-axis to turbine whine.
3️⃣ Spokane falls downtown: mist kills lenses—carry dry microfiber in pocket, not pannier.
4️⃣ Apple blossom drift: allergies hit riders harder at 100 km/h—note if VO sounds nasal.
5️⃣ Wheat wave shots: shutter speed choice explains ‘frozen’ vs ‘flowing’ stalks.
6️⃣ Expo ’74 legacy: cite environmental theme factually—avoid uncritical greenwashing.
25.hook: Eastern Washington looks empty until you read the flood scars—then every coulee tells a time machine story.
25.storyArc: Plateau geometry + basalt columns. Optional dam module with scale humans. Spokane falls urban hydrology. Close: apple economy as tonnage, not postcard.
talkingPoints: Scablands Bretz hypothesis—brief, victorious science arc.
Grand Coulee generation ranking—verify current world list year.
Bing Crosby one fact, correctly sourced.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Lochsa corridor: wildlife dusk peaks—deer kinematics differ from elk.
2️⃣ Lolo Pass Continental Divide: water drop experiment ethical? Use map instead to avoid litter.
3️⃣ Nez Perce history: pronounce carefully; cite 1877 retreat mileage if quoted.
4️⃣ Pavement history 1962: last paved—use as infrastructure timeline, not brag.
5️⃣ River audio: stereo pair if possible—rushing water masks bike issues in post.
6️⃣ Night camp bear protocol—Utah/Idaho/Montana differences one sentence.
26.hook: Lewis and Clark nearly starved here—your fuel gauge doesn’t make you smarter, just luckier in era.
26.storyArc: US-12 as engineering lag narrative. River parallel as sound design. Summit sign as watershed fact. Montana valley as relief.
talkingPoints: Journal quote on colt supper—date and context.
Nez Perce War geography—accuracy over drama.
Wilderness access ethics—no drone in wilderness.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Bison jam: engine off, stay on bike unless charged—every year someone gets gored for a reel.
2️⃣ Supervolcano: avoid ‘due’ without timeframe—USGS prefers recurrence intervals with huge error bars.
3️⃣ Gallatin corridor wind: open ranch gates blow shut—mind ditches.
4️⃣ Fly-fishing iconography: don’t fake casts—permission + skill.
5️⃣ Altitude fueling: note any high-idle hang—carb jetting teaching moment.
6️⃣ Grizzly country: carry spray where legal; say where you stowed it—model behavior.
27.hook: Yellowstone’s ground breathes—your seismometer is storytelling discipline, not clickbait.
27.storyArc: Big sky metrics. Bison encounter as safety protocol episode. Supervolcano explained without apocalypse trailer voice. Park entrance anticipation with rules screengrab.
talkingPoints: Bison near-extinction arithmetic—population trough and recovery.
A River Runs Through It—film vs book vs river.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Geyser basins: stay on boardwalk—thin crust breaks kill people; film the warning signs.
2️⃣ Old Faithful: eruption window posted—show the estimate board, teach uncertainty.
3️⃣ Grand Prismatic overlook vs deck—temperature bacteria color zones need both scales.
4️⃣ Wildlife distance NPS rules: 100 yards bison, 25 yards elk—quote current year.
5️⃣ Hayden Valley traffic: plan pullouts early; overheating bikes happen in jam.
6️⃣ Hydrogen sulfide low areas: if you feel headache, move upwind—don’t tough-guy it.
28.hook: Half the planet’s geysers sit here—treat thermal ground like live ordnance.
28.storyArc: Geothermal act structure: predictability (Old Faithful), chaos (steam fields), color science (Prismatic). Wildlife act with lens length ethics. Canyon module as erosion math.
talkingPoints: Geyser plumbing basics—reservoir, constriction, steam flash.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Beartooth snow in June: ice in shade—ride tires cold first corners slow.
2️⃣ 10,947 ft: fuel injection altitude trim OK; carbs may need pilot tweak—log symptoms.
3️⃣ Kuralt quote: verify wording source—documentary standard.
4️⃣ Red Lodge descent: brake cooling stops—smell is a sensor.
5️⃣ Wyoming wind after: crosswind transition day—gear lean visible to teach.
6️⃣ Oxygen saturation optional: pulse ox on camera if you own one—otherwise skip gimmick.
29.hook: Highest paved road in the Lower 48 is not a trophy—it’s a weather system you ride through.
29.storyArc: Route choice honesty (Cooke vs Chief Joseph). Climb as temperature + pressure overlay. Plateau as tundra silence. Descent as relief + fatigue. Powell night as logistics.
talkingPoints: Tundra growth rate mm/yr—cite tundra ecology text.
Red Lodge mining immigrant layers—specific groups.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Crosswind sail area: panniers high—consider body lean demonstration at safe speed only.
2️⃣ Wind turbine rows: turbulence behind rotors—don’t linger parallel.
3️⃣ Coal trains: draft suck surprises—pass with margin.
4️⃣ Gillette: ask before filming industrial sites—corporate security culture.
5️⃣ Hydration on windy cool days: thirst lags—timer-based sips.
6️⃣ Dust devils: steer through base, not fight top—stability physics.
30.hook: Wyoming’s emptiness is full of wind turbines, coal trains, and votes—film the infrastructure, not the postcard.
30.storyArc: Plains as renewable vs fossil tableau. Women’s suffrage first—political science beat. Gillette as work town portrait without poverty tourism. Close: Black Hills approach foreshadow.
talkingPoints: Wind potential ranking—EIA or NREL citation.
Riding technique: countersteer in gusts basics.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Needles tunnels: measure bar width + bags; walk through first if uncertain.
2️⃣ Mirror fold technique: practice before camera rolls—efficiency, not panic.
3️⃣ Black Hills sacred land: Rushmore photography—context Lakota claims + unclaimed judgment fund.
4️⃣ Bison in park: rut season distance larger—NPS updates seasonally.
5️⃣ Iron Mountain Road pigtail bridges: oncoming sight lines—horn protocol.
6️⃣ Camp pine duff: stake holding power—test pull before dark.
31.hook: A governor built this road to refuse a sculpture—now both exist. Hold two truths without flattening either.
31.storyArc: Granite spires geology. Tunnel sequence as technical motorcycle puzzle. Rushmore as contested monument—facts + Supreme Court case. Custer bison as traffic hazard training.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ 600 km slab: vision narrows with fatigue—20-minute micro stops beat heroic nonstop.
2️⃣ Missouri bridge: expansion joints snap steering—loose grip, neutral throttle.
3️⃣ Minnesota lake glare: polarizer + helmet tint stacking—watch exposure.
4️⃣ Mayo campus: filming exteriors OK; patients deserve privacy—no ER B-roll.
5️⃣ Odometer storytelling: show trip total—continuity for series viewers.
6️⃣ Plains audio: constant wind; plan music bed or heavy VO processing.
32.hook: Flat miles are cognitive experiment—boredom is data; don’t cut it all out.
32.storyArc: Missouri crossing hydrology. Minnesota limnology shout. Rochester as company town medicine. Mental game as honest VO with timestamps.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Driftless hills: gravel on blind corners—county maintenance varies weekly.
2️⃣ Dairy air: flies in July—visor protocol.
3️⃣ Cheese stats: cite USDA or state ag year—avoid round ‘25%’ without year.
4️⃣ Oconomowoc spelling: on-screen graphic—payoff joke lands only if accurate.
5️⃣ Lake effect microclimate: evening wind shift on water—tent orientation.
6️⃣ Milwaukee beer history: one brand date, not a crawl.
33.hook: Glaciers missed this pocket—your suspension feels it before your brain names it.
33.storyArc: Driftless as unglaciated island. Dairy landscape as economy. Town name etymology. Great Lakes preview as bathymetry teaser.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ Third Coast: freshwater refracts differently than ocean—white balance notes.
2️⃣ Benton Harbor / St. Joseph: income disparity story—cite ACS 5-year, name year.
3️⃣ Border crossing home: have goods declaration ready—serial-import gear lists help.
4️⃣ Blue Water Bridge: commercial lane vs car—motorcycle placement ask officer hand signal.
5️⃣ Canada re-entry: metric signs return—film speedo unit switch if applicable.
6️⃣ Host lunch audio: room treatment or accept noisy documentary truth.
34.hook: You’re closing a loop through fresh water, inequality, and a border you now understand differently.
34.storyArc: Lake Michigan scale facts. Host segment as human geography. Fruit belt agriculture climate. Port Huron crossing procedure. Ontario night as emotional restraint—no montage overdose.
talkingPoints: Windsor latitude south of Chicago—verify coordinates.
Trade volume Blue Water—round to year.
Jul 4 border traffic pattern—anecdote + CBSA pattern disclaimer.

fieldGuide: 1️⃣ 401 merge physics: highest AADT in North America—shoulder checks are survival, not style.
2️⃣ Toronto skyline approach: smog/haze summer—PM2.5 optional on-screen if available.
3️⃣ Finale VO: write script, don’t improv—emotion needs structure to land.
4️⃣ Helmet final shot: light the face; silhouette reads as anonymous.
5️⃣ Land acknowledgment: research local nation for your actual home address—specific beats generic.
6️⃣ Credits: list music licenses and map sources—documentary hygiene builds trust.
35.hook: The same driveway. A different odometer. If you learned nothing measurable, you weren’t paying attention.
35.storyArc: Morning departure meter. 401 as stress test. Skyline as closure geometry. Garage scene with maintenance list tease. Sit-down finale: three lessons, one regret, one metric (cost, weight lost, countries), next project hint.
talkingPoints: DR650 reliability data—personal log only, not universal claim.
What you’d change—tool and route, not ‘be braver.’
